deployment_question: How does the model perform on a target domain absent from training?
definition: Train on one or more source domains and evaluate on an unseen domain. This samples a named low-overlap domain point rather than estimating the full continuous similarity curve.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: same-domain train overlap is zero for the held-out domain

implementation_recipe: Define domains from acquisition setting, environment, source dataset, language, subject population, or task context.
For each fold, hold out one domain as test and train only on other domains.
Report per-domain performance rather than only an average.
quality_gates: Separate validation-domain model selection from test-domain reporting.
Report worst-domain performance and performance gaps.
Measure whether held-out domains are actually dissimilar under the chosen SPECTRA axis.
failure_modes: Test-domain leakage through pretraining, normalization, or model selection can invalidate the operating point.
A domain label may bundle multiple shifts and obscure causal interpretation.
Average performance can hide a catastrophic target domain.

citations.title: In Search of Lost Domain Generalization
citations.evidence: Standardizes domain generalization evaluation and emphasizes model selection under unseen-domain testing.

citations.title: NICO++: Towards Better Benchmarking for Domain Generalization
citations.evidence: Introduces a benchmark with many domains to evaluate domain generalization capability.
